markets.question: Will ForecastEx self-certify sports event contracts by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: A federal appeals court ruling on April 7 affirmed the CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction over sports event contracts traded on designated contract markets, preempting state gambling laws and removing a major barrier for DCM self-certifications. This follows the CFTC's March 12 Staff Advisory 26-08, which encouraged DCMs to engage sports leagues like MLB—now formalized via MOU—prior to listing such prediction market contracts, while highlighting manipulation risks. CME Group has already self-certified offerings, signaling viability amid an advance notice of proposed rulemaking with comments due by late April. Traders eye the June 30 deadline as additional DCMs weigh regulatory clarity against lingering state litigation, potentially expanding legal sports outcome derivatives.

markets.question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?
markets.description:
On October 9, 2025 Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Gaza ceasefire, effective since October 2025 under a U.S.-brokered deal, faces growing strain from mutual violations and stalled Phase II talks over Hamas disarmament and post-war governance. The IDF reported 14 Palestinian terrorist ceasefire breaches from April 8–16, followed by five more April 17–21, prompting Israeli operations while Hamas rejected a full disarmament clause on April 16 but signaled openness to partial weapons surrender. U.S.-Hamas direct talks on April 15 advanced the fragile truce amid regional de-escalation with Iran and Lebanon ceasefires, refocusing pressure on Gaza. Traders monitor upcoming negotiations and violation trends for escalation risks, with no formal cancellation yet but rising tensions as of late April.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices USA slightly ahead at 36% implied probability to win Group D over Türkiye at 35%, reflecting a razor-thin contest driven by the co-host's home-soil advantages—including an opener against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium—clashing with Türkiye's surging form from their March 31 playoff triumph over Kosovo. The USMNT's recent friendlies showed mixed results, beating Australia and Paraguay 2-1 but falling 2-1 to Türkiye, underscoring stylistic matchups where USA's athletic depth meets Türkiye's technical flair from stars like Hakan Çalhanoğlu, Kenan Yıldız, and Arda Güler. Paraguay's CONMEBOL-honed defensive grit (16.5%) and Australia's physical AFC pressing (8.2%) add upset potential, keeping the race for top-two advancement—and possible third-place knockout berth—fiercely competitive amid clean injury reports and intensifying base camps.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: FURIA vs GamerLegion - Map 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 2 match between FURIA and GamerLegion in the BLAST Rivals Group B, initially scheduled for April 29 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win Map 1 against GamerLegion.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion win Map 1 against FURIA.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: FURIA vs GamerLegion (BO3) - BLAST Rivals Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 2 match between FURIA and GamerLegion in the BLAST Rivals Group B, initially scheduled for April 29 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win the match against GamerLegion.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion win the match against FURIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: FURIA enters BLAST Rivals Spring 2026 Group B upper bracket as the clear favorite over GamerLegion in this double-elimination BO3 at Fort Worth's Dickies Arena, where top three teams advance to playoffs—NAVI already leads after 2-0 over FaZe on day one. Ranked #3 worldwide with a pristine 3-0 head-to-head edge, FURIA leverages elite form from 4th at IEM Rio 2026 and ESL Pro League playoffs, powered by KSCERATO's 1.19 rating and YEKINDAR's integration. GamerLegion (#31) holds a 70% three-month win rate but falters versus top squads, with recent losses to G2 and BetBoom post-ash's April 23 departure; map veto sets Inferno (GL pick), FURIA's Nuke, and Mirage decider.

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Recent uploads, including the latest scoring just 37.9 million first-day views—ranking 16th among the last 50—reflect diminished viral momentum, with four-day counts sliding from 119 million to 56 million amid content quality critiques and a strategic pivot to Amazon Prime Video's Beast Games, whose season 2 wrapped in February. No blockbuster stunts or collaborations are announced, echoing prior Polymarket resolutions like March and April's "No" outcomes. eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Fire FC holds trader consensus at 55% implied probability to win at home against FC Cincinnati, driven by their third-place Eastern Conference standing (17 points from nine matches) and strong recent form, including a dramatic 3-3 draw at TQL Stadium on April 18 where Hugo Cuypers scored twice. FC Cincinnati languishes around 10th (12 points from 10 games, -4 goal difference), hampered by a crippling defensive injury crisis with seven players out—including center backs Matt Miazga, Miles Robinson, and Teenage Hadebe, plus midfielder Obinna Nwobodo—exacerbating their league-worst away record with zero road wins. Chicago's healthier squad, bolstered by home advantage at Soldier Field, positions them as the slight favorite in this closely contested matchup, though Cincinnati's attacking threats like Evander keep the draw viable at 21.5%.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

Lula's 19% odds for second reflect volatility from undecided voters and his high rejection rates amid economic pressures. markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

events.title: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus implies a near-certain no divorce announcement by June 30, driven by the complete absence of any official statements, legal filings, or credible reports from Bill or Hillary Clinton in recent months, despite early 2026 tabloid speculation fueled by House Oversight Committee depositions on Jeffrey Epstein ties. The couple, wed since 1975 and marking their 50th anniversary last October, quashed rumors with a public date night in New York City on March 10 amid photo leaks from the hearings. No developments have emerged in the past 30 days, underscoring their history of enduring scandals. Realistic shifts would require a late-breaking personal announcement, health crisis, or unforeseen legal pressure before the deadline.

markets.question: Donovan Mitchell: Points O/U 26.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 29 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donovan Mitchell scores more than 26.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Donovan Mitchell scores 26.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Raptors vs. Cavaliers
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 29 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Raptors win, the market will resolve to "Raptors".
If the Cavaliers win, the market will resolve to "Cavaliers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Eastern Conference first-round series tied 2-2, Game 5 shifts back to Cleveland where the fourth-seeded Cavaliers hold home-court advantage against the fifth-seeded Raptors. Toronto's momentum stems from blowout Game 3 (126-104) and gritty Game 4 victory (93-89) at home, where Brandon Ingram and Scottie Barnes combined for 46 points amid stifling defense that cooled Donovan Mitchell and James Harden. Raptors guard Immanuel Quickley remains out with a right hamstring strain, thinning their backcourt, while Cavaliers injury report is clean despite Sam Merrill nursing a minor issue. Expect heightened focus on Jarrett Allen's involvement and Cleveland's supporting cast to reclaim series lead amid playoff intensity.

markets.question: Will A Just Russia – For Truth (SRZP) win the second-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) for second-most seats in the September 2026 State Duma election at 34.5%, closely trailed by New People (NL) at 31% and Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR) at 26.5%, reflecting a tight contest among systemic opposition parties behind dominant United Russia. Recent VCIOM polls through mid-April show NL surging to 13.4% in proportional list voting intentions, overtaking LDPR (10.1%) and matching KPRF (10.9%), fueled by party conventions and Kremlin-backed promotion as a middle-class alternative amid economic pressures eroding ER support. KPRF holds an edge via historical strength in single-member districts, but regional arrests of its activists and LDPR's nationalist appeals keep the race fragmented. Separation could arise from federal party list approvals, electronic primaries, or shifts in undecided voters (10-20%) ahead of the campaign launch.

events.title: Como 1907 vs. SSC Napoli - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Serie A game, scheduled for May 2 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SSC Napoli, defending Serie A champions and second in the standings with around 66 points after 30 matches, visit surging fourth-placed Como 1907 on May 3 at Stadio Giuseppe Sinigaglia. Como's fairy-tale season features robust home form—nine wins in 16 league games—and unbeaten recent head-to-heads against Napoli, including a 2-1 league victory earlier this term, a 0-0 draw, and a dramatic Coppa Italia quarterfinal penalty win in February. Napoli boast strong away results but grapple with injuries to Romelu Lukaku (muscle, late April), Giovanni Di Lorenzo (knee), Amir Rrahmani (thigh), and Antonio Vergara (foot), potentially disrupting their lineup amid a tight Champions League race. Both enter unbeaten in their last three Serie A outings.

markets.question: Will FURIA win CBLOL 2026 Split 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Campeonato Brasileiro de League of Legends (CBLOL) 2026 Split 1.

markets.question: Microsoft (MSFT) Up or Down on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on April 30, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on April 30, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 200+ posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

This follows similar March strikes amid the broader US-Iran conflict, driven by Iran's Strait of Hormuz restrictions and President Trump's ultimatums threatening energy infrastructure if shipping lanes remain blocked. markets.question: Will the Republicans win the South Carolina Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm South Carolina U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have achieved incremental advances in the Pokrovsk direction of Donetsk Oblast over the past two weeks, reaching the outskirts of Vasylivka as of April 24 amid intensified assaults around Hryshyne and Novooleksandrivka, according to open-source battlefield mapping. Ukrainian defenders continue contesting these gains through drone strikes, artillery fire, and counter-infiltrations, with no confirmed Russian entry into Vasylivka per recent Institute for the Study of War assessments up to April 27. Trader sentiment reflects the slow, grinding nature of frontline movements, where small positional shifts matter; upcoming Western military aid deliveries and potential Russian manpower reinforcements could tip the balance before the market's resolution window.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70% victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26 secured the gubernatorial endorsement and automatic primary ballot placement, propelling trader consensus to an 83% implied probability for the September 1 Republican primary winner. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly qualified with 15.5% delegate support, positioning him as the sole remaining challenger at 13.4%, while former Housing Secretary Mike Kennealy's 14% share fell short of the 15% threshold, effectively ending his campaign and dropping his odds to 2%. This consolidation reflects the weight of party backing in a low-turnout primary open to unenrolled voters, with no recent polls contradicting Minogue's frontrunner status amid fundraising edges from his biotech executive background.

markets.question: Will Chelsea Clinton be the democratic nominee for NY-12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the open Democratic primary for New York's 12th Congressional District to succeed retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, trader consensus positions Assemblyman Micah Lasher as the frontrunner at 44.5% implied probability, driven by establishment endorsements including Gov. Kathy Hochul's April 13 backing, Nadler's support, and a planned $5 million Bloomberg super PAC infusion. Assemblyman Alex Bores trails at 33%, buoyed by grassroots and labor ties plus a March internal poll showing him at 19% amid 28% undecideds. Jack Schlossberg garners 18% on Kennedy family name recognition and social media buzz, leading that same poll at 22%, but lacks institutional muscle. Recent April 15 and 20 candidate forums at 92nd Street Y highlighted the crowded field's dynamics, with the June 23 primary looming as high undecideds keep odds fluid.

markets.question: Will Yoo Dong-soo win the 2026 Incheon mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Incheon mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Incheon, South Korea on June 3, 2026 to elect the next mayor of Incheon. 

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Park Chan-dae commands 94.5% trader consensus in the Incheon mayoral race due to his commanding leads in recent polls, including a March survey showing 54.1% support against incumbent Yoo Jeong-bok's 27.9%, bolstered by the Democratic Party's 32-point advantage over the People Power Party in Incheon. As a three-term lawmaker and party-nominated frontrunner aligned with progressive leadership, Park's April 22 official campaign launch—pledging AI, biotech, K-culture, and job creation in Songdo—has solidified momentum amid regional economic priorities. This reflects skin-in-the-game bets on DPK's regional strength five weeks before the June 3 local election. Upsets could arise from scandals, a conservative national swing, Yoo's incumbent advantages in turnout, or undecided voter shifts.

markets.question: Will Google (GOOGL) close at $340-$345 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Google (GOOGL) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Google (GOOGL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an implied 78% probability that Alphabet (GOOGL) shares will close the week of April 27 above $360, propelled by yesterday's blockbuster Q1 2026 earnings where Google Cloud revenue surpassed $20 billion—topping estimates—and backlog swelled amid surging AI demand, with Gemini Enterprise users up 40% quarter-over-quarter. Shares hit a 52-week high of $355.79 intraday on April 29, reflecting a +3% pop from the prior close of $349.78, buoyed by a 5% dividend hike to 22 cents and analyst targets averaging $357 amid upgrades to $400+. Lower bins like $340-$345 at 8% capture residual caution on broader market volatility, but cloud momentum and $4.2 trillion market cap underscore upside conviction ahead of Friday's close.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 20°C or below on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors a 27°C high in Shenzhen on May 1 at 35% implied probability, closely trailed by 26°C at 27.5% and 28°C at 20.5%, reflecting short-range forecast uncertainty amid improving post-rain conditions. Latest updates from sources like The Weather Channel project a cloudy high of 81°F (27°C) with low precipitation risk, following heavy rain and gusty winds through April 29 that dipped temperatures and are easing by April 30 per local meteorological alerts. Ensemble models such as GFS and ECMWF show convergence around 26–28°C, influenced by persistent cloud cover suppressing solar heating despite seasonal norms near 28°C; full clearing could push toward 28°C, while lingering showers favor 26°C. New China Meteorological Administration advisories expected overnight may refine this ahead of Labor Day.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding 94.5% implied probability for a 20-25% margin of victory in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election stems from post-April 16 vote tallies showing her lead at 20.7% as of April 24, bolstered by late-counted provisional and mail ballots that flipped key areas like Passaic County's portion—unprecedented for modern Democrats there. The safely Democratic district, where Kamala Harris led by roughly 12 points in 2024, saw Mejia overperform amid low-turnout conditions typical of House specials favoring incumbents. Weaker Republican turnout against challenger Joe Hathaway further entrenched her position. Realistic challenges include substantial remaining GOP-leaning ballots or certification disputes, though trends point to stability above 20%.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: ShindeN vs MIBR Academy - Map 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket round 3 match between ShindeN and MIBR Academy in the ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "ShindeN" if ShindeN win Map 1 against MIBR Academy.

This market will resolve to "MIBR Academy" if MIBR Academy win Map 1 against ShindeN.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: ShindeN vs MIBR Academy (BO3) - ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket round 3 match between ShindeN and MIBR Academy in the ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "ShindeN" if ShindeN win the match against MIBR Academy.

This market will resolve to "MIBR Academy" if MIBR Academy win the match against ShindeN.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: ShindeN enters the ESL Challenger League Season 51 South America Cup 4 lower bracket round 3 BO3 matchup against MIBR Academy with stronger momentum after a 2-0 win over GameHunters on April 28, rebounding from a 0-2 upper quarterfinals loss to Isurus, following an opening 2-1 victory against Procyon. Ranked #48 globally with a 70.5% win rate over the last three months and strong map performances on Inferno (82%) and Nuke (78%), ShindeN holds a 2-1 head-to-head edge. MIBR Academy, at #137 with a 46.4% win rate, advanced via 2-1 over Fluxo, 0-2 loss to Galorys, and 2-0 against LP, relying on mid-tier map stats like 60% on Ancient. In this double-elimination playoff, the winner advances toward a $17,500 prize pool share, with no reported roster changes or stand-in issues.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner leads trader consensus at 65% implied probability for the 2026 Roland Garros men's singles title after capturing the Monte-Carlo Masters crown two weeks ago, defeating Carlos Alcaraz in the final to reclaim the ATP No. 1 ranking and post a 75% career clay win rate, signaling improved red-clay prowess amid the ongoing swing. Alcaraz's recent wrist injury withdrawal from the tournament—announced last week—along with skips at Barcelona, Madrid, and Rome, has cratered his chances to 1.5%, clearing Sinner's path despite the Spaniard's defending status. Alexander Zverev sits second at 10% with strong 2026 clay form (9-2 record) and historical deep runs at Roland Garros, while Novak Djokovic's lingering shoulder injury—forcing absences from Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid—caps him at 8.8% despite his 24 clay majors. Khachanov and Medvedev share 4.2% on recent momentum, but the field remains wide open with the draw pending and three weeks until May 18 start.

Teammate Michael Olise bolsters Bayern's case at 17%, with 15 goals and 26 assists highlighting his elite creativity on the right wing amid recent power rankings elevating both. Kylian Mbappé's 24 La Liga goals for Real Madrid slip to 12.5% as Madrid trails in the title race, while Ousmane Dembélé's 10 goals and 6 assists in Ligue 1 propel PSG's bid at 11.3%; Lamine Yamal drops to 8.5% post-injury. The June World Cup looms as the decider.

markets.question: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire extended by President Trump since early April, US-Iran diplomatic efforts remain stalled following inconclusive direct talks in Islamabad around April 11-14, where Vice President Vance led the US delegation. Iran recently proposed prioritizing reopening the Strait of Hormuz and lifting sea sanctions before addressing nuclear issues, with the US reviewing this plan as of April 27 amid Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi's Moscow visit. Backchannel communications continue via Pakistan, but no new meeting is confirmed, reflecting trader caution over escalation risks versus de-escalation incentives like permanent peace talks. Upcoming envoy dispatches to Islamabad could catalyze progress before any resolution deadline.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 29°C on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a highest temperature of 29°C in São Paulo on April 29, 2026, reflecting near-unanimous alignment with the latest forecasts from Brazil's Instituto Nacional de Meteorologia (INMET) and supporting models, which project a daily maximum of 28–29°C amid clearing skies following a cold front's departure. Observational data shows morning lows around 19°C rising under moderate northwest winds and high pressure, consistent with late-April climatology where highs typically reach 24–26°C but have trended warmer this week due to reduced cloud cover. This positioning is backed by hourly updates indicating peak afternoon heat near 29°C at key stations like Congonhas Airport. Realistic challenges include isolated late-day showers suppressing the peak or microclimate variations at measurement sites, though current trajectories make deviations unlikely as the day progresses toward evening observations.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 79% implied probability to win the most seats among the 54 up for renewal in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, driven by its ascent to the largest Senate bloc in January 2026 via switches and further expansion to 101 Chamber deputies after the April 3 party window closed. Recent state polls from March (Paraná Pesquisas, Quaest, Datafolha) highlight PL pre-candidates like Michelle Bolsonaro (36% in DF), Cláudio Castro (23% in RJ), and Carlos Bolsonaro (21% in SC) leading or contending, signaling broad right-wing momentum amid Flávio Bolsonaro's tight presidential race with Lula. Smaller parties like NOVO (12.6%) and UNIÃO Brasil (11.1%) draw support as all their seats face full renewal, while PT trails at 2.6% amid weaker showings.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects intense parity in the 2026 WNBA championship market, with New York Liberty (24.5%), Indiana Fever (21.5%), and Dallas Wings (20.9%) clustered tightly atop implied probabilities due to transformative offseason moves. The April 14 draft elevated Dallas with No. 1 pick Azzi Fudd reuniting alongside Paige Bueckers in the backcourt, while Seattle Storm added international center Awa Fam Thiam at No. 3 and Washington Mystics grabbed Lauren Betts at No. 4, spreading elite rookie talent. Indiana's healthy core of Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston, and Kelsey Mitchell fuels Fever optimism after injury-plagued prior campaigns, and New York bolstered their roster with three-time All-Star Satou Sabally. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces re-signed A'ja Wilson but trail amid league-wide elevation from free agency and the April 3 expansion draft for Portland Fire and Toronto Tempo, which redistributed depth and heightened competition across 16 teams.

markets.question: Will Adam Miller win the 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election will be held on June 2, 2026, to elect the mayor of Los Angeles, California. If no candidate receives a majority of the vote, a runoff election will be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the City of Los Angeles.

events.description:

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely watched 2026 Los Angeles mayoral primary on June 2, trader consensus favors City Councilmember Nithya Raman at 58% implied probability, reflecting her late-entry momentum as a progressive challenger and lead in a controversial late-March Loyola Marymount poll amid incumbent Mayor Karen Bass's 56% unfavorability rating. Recent ethics filings show Raman and reality TV outsider Spencer Pratt outpacing Bass in fundraising over the past month, boosting Pratt to 19.5% as an anti-establishment alternative criticizing elite politicians via viral ads. A UCLA Luskin poll last week highlighted 40% undecided voters, underscoring volatility with Bass at 20%, as no candidate nears a runoff-avoiding majority.

markets.question: Will Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 10, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Al Ittihad at 64% implied probability in this Saudi Pro League clash at Al Shabab's home, driven by their superior sixth-place standing versus Al Shabab's 12th, unbeaten run in the last five head-to-heads including a 2-0 win in December 2025, and stronger recent form with four victories in six league games. Al Shabab's competitiveness at 49.5% reflects home advantage and resilience in mid-table battles, though injuries to key midfielder Vincent Sierro and defender Mohammed Harbush weaken their lineup. Draw priced at 20% accounts for tight contests, but Al Ittihad's squad depth—despite recent suspensions for Danilo and Moussa Diaby lifting post their April 28 Al-Taawoun match—bolsters their edge amid no major new injury disruptions in the past 48 hours.

markets.question: Palantir (PLTR) Up or Down on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on April 30, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on April 30, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.PLTR%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.PLTR%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party at 92.5% implied probability for the IL-09 House seat, driven by the district's longstanding Democratic stronghold status—encompassing liberal North Shore suburbs like Evanston and Rogers Park with a Cook Partisan Voting Index rating it solidly blue. Longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky's May 2025 retirement opened the race, but former state Sen. Daniel Biss's March 17, 2026, primary victory over 15 rivals solidified a strong nominee with robust fundraising and local endorsements. Weak Republican opposition, including low-profile nominee John Elleson, reinforces this positioning absent national midterm dynamics. Potential shifts could arise from late scandals, health issues, or a dramatic GOP wave, though historical precedents in D+25 districts suggest high barriers to flipping. The general election is November 3, 2026.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) close above $220 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Apple (AAPL) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AAPL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple (AAPL) shares closed April 29 at $270.17, down 0.20% in a day's range of $267.04–$271.04, reflecting pre-earnings caution ahead of fiscal Q2 results scheduled for release after market close on April 30 at 5 p.m. EDT. Trader consensus anticipates robust revenue growth of 10.6% year-over-year, fueled by strong iPhone 17 shipments and services segment expansion, with EPS estimates rising 9.1%. News of CEO Tim Cook's planned exit and John Ternus's succession starting September introduces mild uncertainty, yet analysts maintain a bullish outlook with an average price target of $297.88 versus the 52-week high of $288.62. Resolution hinges on April 30 intraday momentum around key technical supports at $267 and resistance near $271.

markets.question: Will Benfica finish 2nd in the Primeira Liga for the 2025-26 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve to the team officially recognized by Primeira Liga as finishing in second place. If multiple teams are officially awarded second place, the market will resolve to the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season (e.g. they are mathematically unable to achieve enough points), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after June 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no 2nd place team declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Primeira Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Benfica at 78.5% implied probability for Primeira Liga runner-up, reflecting their unbeaten record (22W-9D-0L, 75 points from 31 games) and three-point lead over Sporting CP (72 points from 30 games) atop the standings behind dominant leaders FC Porto (82 points). Benfica's pivotal 2-1 away victory over Sporting on April 19 propelled them ahead, showcasing superior head-to-head form amid Sporting's recent draw-heavy streak. With three matches left—Benfica facing Estrela da Amadora away, Famalicão at home, and Braga away—versus Sporting's trip to Nacional, home vs. Rio Ave, and daunting Porto finale, traders see Benfica's defensive solidity and goal difference (+47) as key edges despite Sporting's game in hand and superior GD (+55). FC Porto's slim 0.9% reflects their unassailable seven-point cushion.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Buenos Aires be 25°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ar/ezeiza/SAEZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Cagliari: Aleksandar Kovacevic vs Marcos Giron
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aleksandar Kovacevic and Marcos Giron in the Cagliari, originally scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 10:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aleksandar Kovacevic' if Aleksandar Kovacevic advances against Marcos Giron.

This market will resolve to 'Marcos Giron' if Marcos Giron advances against Aleksandar Kovacevic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin edge for Aleksandar Kovacevic at 50% implied probability in this ATP Challenger Cagliari round-of-16 clay matchup against eighth seed Marcos Giron (ATP No. 76 to Kovacevic's No. 97), balancing their modest career clay records—Kovacevic 24-33 lifetime, Giron 36-50. Both notched straight-sets wins in the round of 32: Giron rallied past higher-ranked Aleksandar Vukic 6-4, 1-6, 6-1 on April 28, showcasing resilience, while Kovacevic grinded out a 6-3, 7-6(6) decision over qualifier Luca Potenza on April 29. Giron leads their lone head-to-head 1-0 from 2023 Indian Wells hardcourt, but first clay meeting evens stylistic matchup between the right-handers. Momentum from Giron's recent challenger final or Kovacevic's Indian Wells upsets over Hurkacz and Moutet keeps it competitive; late injury reports or outdoor weather shifts could tip probabilities.

markets.question: Will "The Great Divide" - Noah Kahan debut week album sales be less than 125k?
markets.description:
Noah Kahan's new album 'The Great Divide' is scheduled to be released on April 24, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the debut week sales for Noah Kahan's album 'The Great Divide', according to Hits Daily Double.

If the album 'The Great Divide' has not been released by May 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.
 
If the album’s debut week sales fall exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the "HITS TOP 50" list found at https://hitsdailydouble.com/sales_plus_streaming, specifically, the figure in the column titled "Activity", once the results are finalized for the album’s debut week. If this resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

markets.question: Will Travis Kelce retire before next season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Travis Kelce retires from the NFL before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement from Kelce that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred, as long as the announced retirement is stated to take effect before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Announcements that Kelce will retire after the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Travis Kelce, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
