markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by ≥1.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

events.title: April Inflation US - Monthly
events.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a tight contest for April 2026 CPI month-over-month at 0.5% (37.5% implied probability) versus 0.6% (33.5%), reflecting expectations of moderation from March's surprise 0.9% surge—driven by a 10.9% gasoline spike amid Iran tensions—while headline year-over-year hit 3.3% and core eased to 2.6%. Key differentiators include post-ceasefire oil declines offsetting potential rises in airfares and used vehicles, alongside sticky shelter costs and rising consumer inflation expectations amid slumping sentiment at a record-low 49.8. High-frequency indicators lean toward 0.2%, but trader capital favors persistence near 0.5-0.6%; resolution hinges on Bureau of Labor Statistics data due May 12.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas holds a dominant edge over Toronto KOI in recent head-to-heads, sweeping their last six series 0-6 with a 18-2 map advantage, capped by a 3-1 elimination win in the CDL Minor I tournament on April 25 where Mercules and Shotzzy delivered hardpoint masterclasses on maps like Scar and Den. Both teams entered Stage 3 Major Qualifiers strongly—Toronto KOI at 2-0 (6-2 map record) after wins over Riyadh Falcons and others, OpTic Texas 1-0 (3-1)—fueled by stable rosters featuring Shotzzy, Dashy, Huke, and Mercules for Texas, and Insight, CleanX, JoeDeceives, Kips for KOI. Recent scrims showed competitiveness (e.g., 4-3, 5-5 map splits), but trader consensus reflects Texas' superior form, meta adaptation on Black Ops 6 maps like Gridlock and Exposure, and momentum heading into this BO5 qualifier bracket match.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Recent viral engagements, like high-volume days exceeding 70 posts amid pop culture buzz and xAI announcements, contrast with quieter periods tied to SpaceX milestones, creating tight competition among 1000-1200 ranges. Key swing factors include potential election-year news cycles or platform algorithm tweaks boosting frequency, with daily averages of 25-40 historically volatile; traders eye early May data for momentum shifts before month-end resolution.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $82,000 on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will Brian King be the Democratic nominee for UT-01?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the UT-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 70.5% implied probability to win Utah's CD-01 Democratic primary on June 23, buoyed by his dominant fundraising—$860,000 cash on hand—strong name recognition from his prior congressional service, and a March Data for Progress poll showing him at 36% among likely primary voters. State Sen. Nate Blouin trails at 26%, bolstered by progressive endorsements but hampered by a resurfaced April controversy over past social media posts mocking Latter-day Saints, prompting an apology and rival Kathleen Riebe's dropout and endorsement of McAdams. The recent state Democratic convention upset, where newcomer Liban Mohamed narrowly edged McAdams via ranked-choice voting, failed to sway markets, as delegates skew activist while broader primary voters favor McAdams' moderate profile in the newly drawn, blue-leaning district.

markets.question: Will Nolan McLean win the 2026 NL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes tops trader consensus at 36% implied probability for the 2026 NL Cy Young as the defending winner, bolstered by MLB's latest poll placing him first among National League starters and a rebound from his rocky season debut to post a 2.48 ERA with a 0.72 WHIP over six starts. Michael King trails closely at 27% after anchoring the Padres rotation with a 2.41 ERA, 34 strikeouts, and .177 opponent batting average in 33.2 innings. Shohei Ohtani's successful pitching return (4-1, strong innings totals) and Eury Pérez's quality starts for Miami fuel their 22-23% shares, while Brewers rookie Jacob Misiorowski's franchise-record 11 Opening Day strikeouts propel his 20% odds in this competitive early-season market defined by ace-level peripherals and workload management.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 275M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US crude oil inventories dropped sharply by 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24, per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, reversing recent builds during spring refinery maintenance and exceeding expectations for a slight increase. This draw reflects record-high exports of 6.4 million barrels per day, refinery inputs rising to 16.1 million barrels per day at 89.6% utilization, and early signals of summer driving season demand. EIA forecasts Brent crude peaking near $115 per barrel in Q2 amid global stock draws, though high US production around 13.6 million barrels per day tempers declines. Weekly EIA reports through June 5 will track these trends, with gasoline and distillate stocks also falling notably last week.

markets.question: Spread: AS Monaco FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AS Monaco FC" if AS Monaco FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Metz".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Metz vs. AS Monaco FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Metz sit 18th in Ligue 1 with 26 points from 30 matches, mired in a relegation scrap, hosting seventh-placed AS Monaco on May 2 at Stade Saint-Symphorien amid a mounting injury crisis including Maxime Colin (calf), Boubacar Traoré (knee), and Believe Munongo out. Metz's porous defense concedes heavily, worsened by recent struggles against bottom-table rivals. Monaco, with 49 points from 29 games, endured a 4-1 loss to Paris FC last week despite Folarin Balogun extending his scoring streak to nine straight appearances and Paul Pogba's injury return, but remain potent away with an unbeaten run in eight head-to-heads versus Metz, capped by September's 5-2 win. Monaco's long-term absentees like Takumi Minamino (knee) and Mohammed Salisu (cruciate) test depth, yet their form and matchup edge shape trader consensus toward visiting superiority in this table-climbing fixture.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Democrats at 60.5% to win the 2028 US presidential election, reflecting historical patterns where no party has secured three consecutive terms since the 1980s amid incumbent fatigue. Recent surges in Democratic odds to all-time highs around mid-April stem from President Trump's persistently low approval ratings and expectations of a Democratic wave in the 2026 midterms, where markets favor Democrats to flip the House (87%) and Senate (55%). Vice President JD Vance's frontrunner status for the Republican nomination has weakened, with his personal odds hitting lows, while an open-seat dynamic post-Trump's term limit adds uncertainty. Key upcoming catalysts include midterm results and early primary polling in swing states.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 3°C on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official observations from Moscow's primary meteorological station record the highest temperature on April 29, 2026, at 3.8°C, driving near-unanimous trader consensus on the 3°C outcome as the market-implied probability reflects this verified data amid a severe late-April cold anomaly. A record 27 mm of precipitation—likely sleet and snow—from a Baltic cyclone on April 27 suppressed maxima to 4.4°C, followed by 1.9°C on April 28 and ongoing cloudy, rainy conditions (3.3 mm on the 29th) with northerly winds holding daytime highs well below the monthly normal of ~11°C, marking an 8.3°C departure. This aligns with Roshydromet station measurements typically used for resolution; only a post hoc official revision upward—such as from an overlooked peripheral sensor—could challenge it, though improbable once finalized.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 79% implied probability to Finland finishing in Eurovision 2026's top 5, propelled by frontrunners Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin"—a bold classical violin-pop fusion that dominated their March national final and has sustained betting dominance through strong previews and fan polls like OGAE. Greece (65%), Israel (61%), France (58%), and Denmark (57%) trail closely, buoyed by charismatic national selection winners and historical televote appeal, while recent OGAE results and stage reveals have nudged Greece upward. With all entries confirmed post-national finals, rehearsals kick off May 2 in Vienna, potentially altering momentum before semis on May 12/14 and the May 16 final—where secret jury votes and live televote surges add inherent volatility.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices an 87% chance against the U.S. enacting a federal AI safety bill before 2027, reflecting stalled legislative progress amid the 119th Congress despite recent introductions like the American Leadership in AI Act on April 27 by Reps. Lieu and Obernolte, which consolidates over 20 governance proposals but faces committee hurdles. The White House's March 20 National Policy Framework urged light-touch federal regulations preempting patchwork state laws—over 600 AI bills introduced at the state level in 2026—yet no bills have advanced to floor votes, echoing historical challenges in passing comprehensive tech legislation. With eight months until resolution, procedural bottlenecks, filibuster risks, and competing priorities like appropriations dim prospects for enactment.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent criticisms of NATO allies for insufficient support amid escalating tensions with Iran have fueled speculation about a potential US withdrawal, with White House spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt confirming discussions on the matter in early April. However, a 2023 law requires two-thirds Senate approval for exit, prompting vows from Democrats like Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and former Speaker Nancy Pelosi to block any move, while even some Republicans urge debate over burden-sharing. On April 29, Trump announced a review of US troop levels in Germany following a call with Putin, pressuring Europe on defense spending without committing to formal withdrawal. Traders price a low 12% implied probability before 2027, reflecting institutional barriers amid ongoing alliance diplomacy.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: MD-06 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the Democratic Party at 87.5% to win Maryland's 6th congressional district House seat, driven by the district's D+3 Cook Partisan Voter Index—the least Democratic-leaning in the state—and Solid Democratic ratings from Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball. Incumbent Rep. April McClain Delaney holds a fundraising edge over self-funded primary challenger David Trone as of late March, leading 49%-38% in a recent Hart Research poll, with endorsements from Gov. Wes Moore, Sen. Chris Van Hollen, and the full Maryland Democratic House delegation. The Republican primary features low-funded contenders like Robin Ficker, echoing 2024's 53%-47% Democratic general victory. No notable developments have emerged in the past 30 days ahead of the June 23 primaries.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 launch in mid-April has solidified trader consensus at 64.5% implied probability for holding the top AI model spot through May's end, dominating LMSYS Chatbot Arena with 1504 Elo and SWE-bench Verified at 82%, outpacing rivals in coding and long-context reasoning. Google trails at 29.5% on Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview's competitive benchmarks, bolstered by recent app enhancements, while OpenAI's GPT-5.4 lags amid stalled release momentum. Lower odds for xAI, Meta, and Chinese labs like DeepSeek reflect benchmark gaps and compute constraints. Traders eye potential May catalysts like Google I/O announcements or surprise frontier model drops, but Anthropic's rapid iteration pace drives the frontrunner status amid tight U.S. lab competition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman dominates trader consensus at 79% implied probability for a GOP hold in WI-06, reflecting the district's solid Republican lean—rated Solid R by Cook Political Report—and his history of double-digit victories, including a 22-point 2024 win. Superior fundraising ($700,000 cash on hand as of March 31 versus top Democrat Bradley Smith's $53,000) bolsters his edge ahead of the August 11 open primaries. A fresh development on April 27 saw Democratic candidate Aaron Wojciechowski exit and endorse Smith, slightly consolidating the crowded eight-candidate Democratic field, yet Democratic odds linger at 28% amid independents like Mike Thurow splitting the anti-Grothman vote. No recent polls exist, but structural factors favor retention.

markets.question: Will U2 perform at the 2026 Todo Mundo no Rio music festival?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artists who perform live and in person at the Todo Mundo no Rio 2026 music festival, currently expected to take place in April or May. Appearances during which the listed artist does not perform will not count towards the resolution of this market.

If this event is cancelled or otherwise delayed beyond July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be footage and information from Todo Mundo no Rio 2026 festival, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for performers at Todo Mundo no Rio 2026 hinges on the official full lineup release on April 29, confirming Shakira as headliner for the free Copacabana Beach mega-concert on May 2, with DJ Vintage Culture opening at 17:45, DJ Maz at 19:00, Shakira taking the stage at 21:45, and a post-show DJ set at 00:15. This third edition of Rio's tourism-boosting initiative—following Madonna and Lady Gaga's record crowds—features major sponsors like Corona and Santander, projecting R$800 million economic impact from 2 million attendees. Posters pairing Shakira with Anitta spark guest spot speculation, while the event's imminent timeline leaves little room for changes amid high anticipation and global streaming plans.

markets.question: Will Trump post "Prosecute" or "Prosecution" on Truth Social this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if @realDonaldTrump posts/truths the listed term between April 27, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, all text posted by the listed account in quote and reply posts/truths count toward a "Yes" resolution, but quoted posts/truths and reposts/reTruths will not count.

Text posted in images, memes, or other non-animated, non-video media that are not strictly text will qualify towards a "Yes" resolution only if the listed term is spelled out clearly and in full. (e.g., words spelled out in a letter posted as a .jpg will qualify, however a word posted as part of an animated .gif will not.)

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: r@d1cal, for "radical") will disqualify it from counting toward a "Yes" resolution. Sigils at the beginnings of words, such as hashtags, "@" symbols, or denotations of currency, will not disqualify a term from counting towards a "Yes" resolution.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

The resolution source for this market will be Donald Trumps's verified Truth Social account: @realDonaldTrump

Please note, only the @realDonaldTrump verified Truth Social account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Donald Trump posts/truths from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's Truth Social posts this week (April 27-May 3) are dominated by escalating tensions with Iran, including a recent confrontation where an Iranian-flagged cargo ship, TOUSKA, attempted to breach the U.S. naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting direct warnings of severe retaliation absent a deal within 48 hours. This follows a pattern of expletive-laden ultimatums, infrastructure threats, and optimistic claims of nearing peace talks amid reports of daily Iranian losses exceeding $500 million. Domestically, a Supreme Court 6-3 ruling against racial gerrymandering drew praise, while midterm campaign endorsements continue via proxies. Traders monitor for recurring phrases like "POTUS" or "book" amid these foreign policy catalysts and potential diplomatic breakthroughs.

markets.question: Will Sweden be the Jury Winner in the Eurovision 2026 Grand Final?

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Australia as the narrow jury frontrunner for Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, with Delta Goodrem's "Eclipse" earning consistent points in ongoing Eurojury simulations from Denmark, Albania, and Switzerland, signaling strong appeal among music professionals for its polished production and veteran staging potential. France trails closely via Monroe's "Regarde," praised in Wiwi Jury reviews for sophisticated composition, while Finland and Denmark lag slightly despite solid precursor nods—Finland's "Liekinheitin" leans more televote-heavy, and Denmark's "Før vi går hjem" faces stiffer ballad competition. With all entries locked post-national finals, rehearsals starting next week and the jury show looming before the May 16 final, staging execution and last-minute tweaks could swing this tight race among jury darlings.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Tel Aviv be 25°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Ben Gurion International Airport in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LLBG

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 65-89 tweets from April 30 to May 2, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 30 12:00 PM ET to May 2, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward 40-64 Elon Musk posts at 45.5% implied probability for April 30-May 2, reflecting his recent moderation to 15-25 daily X updates amid SpaceX's Falcon Heavy launch on April 29, which sparked multiple high-engagement reposts and comments today. Trackers like Kaggle data show mid-April averages around 13 posts per day with spikes to 26, while prior weekly markets resolved at 25-35 daily amid pop culture and political buzz—now quieter without major catalysts like album drops or viral controversies. The 65-89 bin trails at 33.5%, as historical patterns rarely exceed 30/day absent breaking news, though an unexpected meme storm or Tesla announcement could swing higher; watch for real-time engagement metrics as the window opens tomorrow.

markets.question: Will Claude go down 3-5 times in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of days in the month of May during which Claude (claude.ai) experiences any downtime.

Claude will be considered to have experienced downtime on a given day if the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/) displays that day’s status box as any color other than green once the day’s status box is finalized. Daily status boxes for Claude components other than claude.ai will not impact this market’s resolution.

A day will be considered finalized once the following day’s status box has a display color other than the grey color used for days which have not yet begun.

This market will resolve once the final day in May 2026 is finalized. If the final day in May 2026 has not been finalized by June 7, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the days in May which have been finalized up to that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors Claude experiencing downtime on 12+ days in May (74.5% implied probability), with 9-11 days at 21%, reflecting deep concerns over Anthropic's infrastructure reliability amid a surge of service disruptions in April 2026. The official Claude status page reports incidents on over 10 days this month, including full claude.ai unavailability on April 28, elevated errors across large language models like Opus 4.7 and Haiku 4.5 on April 29, and repeated API failures, billing glitches, and code review issues. This pattern—despite 98.69% 90-day uptime—stems from scaling challenges during rapid model updates and surging demand, eroding trader confidence without announced fixes. Key to watch: Anthropic engineering postmortems or capacity expansions before May's end.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 83% implied probability to no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the FOMC's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid balanced risks, as Chair Powell emphasized "nobody's calling for a hike right now." March CPI surged to 3.3% year-over-year—driven by a 10.9% energy spike from geopolitical tensions—pushing back rate-cut expectations to late 2026 and briefly elevating hike odds via CME FedWatch to around 17%, yet the March dot plot median still projects slight easing to 3.4% by year-end. Labor market softening, with unemployment forecasts at 4.4%, reinforces trader consensus for steady or accommodative policy; watch May nonfarm payrolls and June FOMC for shifts.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Spring splits across major regions entering playoffs as of late April 2026, trader consensus in the MSI 2026 qualification market reflects frontrunners' dominant regular-season form and favorable paths to the 11-team field in Daejeon, South Korea (June 28-July 12). Bilibili Gaming leads LPL Split 2 standings, bolstered by their First Stand win granting a Bracket Stage bye, while LEC contenders Karmine Corp and G2 Esports advanced via recent playoff victories like KC's 2-0 over GIANTX. GenG tops LCK early Road to MSI indicators, with T1 close behind. LCS and LCP races remain tighter amid roster tweaks, but double-elimination formats leave room for upsets before late June cutoffs. Momentum from patch adaptations and head-to-head edges drives current sentiment.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - April 30, 2:50PM-2:55PM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Hakeem Jeffries' dominant incumbency advantage and massive fundraising lead—$5.4 million cash on hand as of late March—anchor trader consensus at 92% odds for a Democratic Party win in solidly blue NY-08 (D+24 PVI), where he captured 75% in the 2024 general election. Jeffries faces only underfunded challenger Vance Bostic in the June 23 Democratic primary after progressive Chi Ossé withdrew, while obscure Republicans Lewis Mizrahi and Richard Simmons vie in their primary with no signs of viability. No recent polls exist, but historical safe-seat precedents and weak GOP performance sustain this positioning. Upsets would require a stunning primary loss, late scandal, health issue, or unprecedented national Republican wave flipping deep-blue districts.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the Fed Pause–Cut–Cut in the next three decisions (Apr–Jun–Jul)?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds rate. The decisions on the target federal funds rate are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings: April 28-29; June 16-17; and July 28-29.

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other". Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors the Federal Reserve maintaining its federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% across the April, June, and July FOMC meetings, with an 84.5% implied probability for Pause-Pause-Pause, reflecting real capital backing the view of policy patience amid persistent inflation risks. Today's FOMC statement confirmed the anticipated April hold, driven by March CPI's 0.9% monthly surge—the hottest since June 2022—fueled by Iran war-related gasoline spikes to $4/gallon, alongside robust March nonfarm payroll gains of 178,000 and low weekly jobless claims near 219,000. February PCE inflation held at 2.8% year-over-year, exceeding the Fed's 2% target, while resilient labor conditions diminish cut urgency. CME FedWatch futures corroborate low June cut odds below 3%, with focus shifting to upcoming April PCE data on April 30 and May CPI for June meeting signals.

markets.question: Will Gençlerbirliği SK win on 2026-05-03?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 3, 2026
If Gençlerbirliği SK wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Tight trader consensus in this Süper Lig relegation scrap reflects both clubs' inconsistent form and injury-hit squads, with Gençlerbirliği edging as slight away favorite at 36% implied probability despite Fatih Karagümrük's home advantage at Atatürk Olimpiyat Stadı. Karagümrük (18th, 21 points) drew goalless with Beşiktaş last weekend but lost three of their prior five, scoring sparingly amid absences like Daniel Johnson, João Camacho, and Moussa Koné (muscle/ligament issues). Gençlerbirliği (15th, 28 points) notched recent wins over Kocaelispor and Galatasaray but slumped with four losses in six, missing Peter Etebo, Moussa Kyabou, and Abdullah Sahindere. Balanced head-to-head (2-2-1) and fragile defenses fuel the draw's 29% viability on May 3.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Nicolás Maduro at 66% to remain Venezuela's official head of state by December 31, 2026, despite his January 3 U.S. capture and ongoing detention in New York awaiting trial on narcotics charges, reflecting constitutional ambiguities limiting interim Vice President Delcy Rodríguez's tenure to 90 days plus potential extensions closing around July. Rodríguez, sworn in by the Supreme Court and backed by military loyalists, holds 20.5% as she consolidates power through purges of Maduro allies and oil deals with the U.S. administration, amid PSUV internal divisions. Opposition leader María Corina Machado at 8.5% gains from popular support and exile advocacy for snap elections, but regime control suppresses transitions; no election timeline exists, with UN recognition of Maduro adding uncertainty to resolution criteria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Perry Johnson's commanding 63% implied probability in the Michigan Republican gubernatorial primary reflects trader consensus on his self-funding capacity and heavy early ad spending, enabling him to dominate visibility despite a recent Detroit Regional Chamber poll showing U.S. Rep. John James leading 37% to Johnson's 20% among likely GOP primary voters. James holds a polling edge from name recognition as a two-term congressman and prior statewide runs, but traders discount this amid Johnson's aggressive tactics, including a lawsuit against James over allegedly unlawful campaign logos filed last week. Lower-tier candidates like Mike Cox and Aric Nesbitt trail far behind, with the August 4 primary four months away and signature validations pending.

markets.question: Will Gainare Tottori win on 2026-04-05?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 5, 2026
If Gainare Tottori wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Gainare Tottori vs. Reilac Shiga
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 5, 2026 between Gainare Tottori and Reilac Shiga.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West B prices a draw at 49.3%, with Gainare Tottori (47.5%) edging Reilac Shiga (38%) in this mid-table matchup at Heiwado HATO Stadium, reflecting evenly matched dynamics amid defensive struggles. Tottori sit 10th on a four-match losing streak across league play, compounded by key absences including forwards Kojo Dadzie (cruciate ligament) and Ibuki Yoshida (Achilles), midfielder Jun Katori (foot), and defender Hayato Nukui (Achilles), yet traders imply resilience against a 7th-placed Shiga side with mixed recent form (L-L-W-W-L) and poor home scoring in recent outings (no goals in two of five). Past head-to-head favors Shiga (5-0 win), but low-scoring trends keep probabilities tightly bunched.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 64-65°F on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Seattle indicate sunny skies and highs near 64-66°F today under a resuming high-pressure ridge, fueling the tight trader consensus between 64-65°F (44% implied probability) and 66-67°F (47.5%) as the leading outcomes. This split reflects subtle model discrepancies in peak afternoon solar insolation versus potential lingering Puget Sound marine stratus, which could moderate boundary-layer warming; full clearing favors the upper range, while partial cloud persistence supports the lower. Historical late-April averages hover around 63°F, with climatological norms amplified by light northerly winds aiding offshore flow. Real-time Sea-Tac Airport observations through late afternoon will resolve the market amid inherent short-range forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong's High Court sentenced pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai to 20 years in prison on February 9, 2026, for conspiracy to collude with foreign forces and sedition under the national security law—the harshest penalty yet—following a trial that began in late 2023. In March, his legal team confirmed no appeal against the conviction or sentence, rendering it final and dashing hopes for judicial reversal, while a separate fraud conviction was overturned without affecting overall custody. With no notable developments in the past 30 days and concurrent shorter non-national security terms potentially concluding by June, traders price near-certainty against release by June 30, citing institutional commitment to the verdict. Realistic shifts could involve medical parole given Lai's age of 78, executive clemency, or diplomatic interventions amid Western calls for his freedom.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the TX-15 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-15 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: In Texas's 15th Congressional District, a Hispanic-majority battleground in the Rio Grande Valley, trader consensus slightly favors Democrat Bobby Pulido at 52% over incumbent Republican Monica De La Cruz at 46.5%, reflecting post-primary momentum from Pulido's 68%-32% March 3 landslide win that showcased strong Democratic turnout. De La Cruz, who unopposed in her primary and holds fundraising advantages with over $2.3 million cash on hand as of late March, leverages her 2024 reelection margin and incumbency in a district where she prevailed 57%-43%. The tight odds stem from Pulido's Tejano celebrity appeal potentially boosting Latino voter engagement, echoing Harris's narrow 2024 presidential edge here, balanced against De La Cruz's proven path-to-victory. Separation could arise from early polls, ad spending surges, endorsements, or national House control dynamics ahead of the November general election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Xiyu Wang as the narrow US Open favorite at 39.4% implied probability ahead of Aryna Sabalenka's 34%, highlighting a tightly contested hard-court futures market amid fluctuating top-seed form. Wang's back-to-back ITF W50 titles in Ma'anshan and Baotou through late April signal resurgent momentum post-injury, boosting her appeal as a tall lefty with prior US Open junior success. Sabalenka, world No. 1 with a dominant 25-1 record including the Indian Wells title over Rybakina, carries slight injury concerns after withdrawing from Stuttgart. Rybakina's Australian Open triumph and fresh Stuttgart clay win keep her viable at 18.7%, while Swiatek's hard-court inconsistencies and recent Madrid illness retirement cap her at 16%. The bunched leaders underscore no runaway favorite in a parity-driven WTA field.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely contested Kansas Republican gubernatorial primary set for August 4, 2026, trader consensus favors Senate President Ty Masterson slightly ahead of former Governor Jeff Colyer and Joy Eakins, reflecting a fragmented field where low voter name recognition and competitive fundraising keep odds tight among established GOP figures. Recent campaign finance reports highlight self-loans from millionaire candidates like Colyer and businessman Philip Sarnecki, fueling ad wars post-January debate that failed to produce a clear frontrunner. Colyer's April 24 endorsement from Arkansas Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders, praising his prior executive record and Trump ties, has narrowed gaps, but Masterson's legislative clout and Colyer's higher early recognition sustain the deadlock. Upcoming June 1 filing deadline, fresh polls, or high-profile endorsements could tip the balance in this open-seat race against Democratic incumbent Laura Kelly.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Kindrativka by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the intersection at 51.141847° N, 34.771637° E in Kindrativka, Sumy Oblast, by the specified date 11:59 PM ET.

The intersection will be considered captured if any part of the intersection is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the specified date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the intersection, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Intersection Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/KS.png

Intersection Location in Kindrativka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/KS2.png

Kindrativka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/KS3.png

Location on Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/BTC6vHHmyYcJ1WETA

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have resumed assaults on the northern and eastern outskirts of Kindrativka in Sumy Oblast, securing incremental advances amid positional fighting as of April 27, per geolocated footage and frontline analyses. This border village, briefly captured by Russia in June 2025 before Ukrainian liberation in late July, exemplifies the attritional cross-border offensive north of Sumy City, where Moscow applies sustained pressure through infantry and artillery. Ukrainian strikes continue targeting Russian positions in southeastern Kindrativka, maintaining a contested status with no full control confirmed. Key factors include troop reinforcements, logistics along the Khotin-Kindrativka road, and potential escalation from nearby Andriivka clashes; daily ISW updates and DeepState maps will signal shifts before the market deadline.

markets.question: Map Handicap: SHIN (-1.5) vs MIBR Academy (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket round 3 match between ShindeN and MIBR Academy in the ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "ShindeN" if ShindeN wins 2 or more maps than MIBR Academy in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "MIBR Academy". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: SHIN (-1.5) vs MIBR Academy (+1.5)

events.title: Counter-Strike: ShindeN vs MIBR Academy (BO3) - ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket round 3 match between ShindeN and MIBR Academy in the ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "ShindeN" if ShindeN win the match against MIBR Academy.

This market will resolve to "MIBR Academy" if MIBR Academy win the match against ShindeN.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: ShindeN enters the ESL Challenger League Season 51 South America Cup 4 lower bracket round 3 BO3 matchup against MIBR Academy with stronger momentum after a 2-0 win over GameHunters on April 28, rebounding from a 0-2 upper quarterfinals loss to Isurus, following an opening 2-1 victory against Procyon. Ranked #48 globally with a 70.5% win rate over the last three months and strong map performances on Inferno (82%) and Nuke (78%), ShindeN holds a 2-1 head-to-head edge. MIBR Academy, at #137 with a 46.4% win rate, advanced via 2-1 over Fluxo, 0-2 loss to Galorys, and 2-0 against LP, relying on mid-tier map stats like 60% on Ancient. In this double-elimination playoff, the winner advances toward a $17,500 prize pool share, with no reported roster changes or stand-in issues.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander as an 85% favorite to win the NY-10 Democratic primary on June 23, reflecting his surging progressive momentum from recent endorsements like 32BJ SEIU on April 2 and Sen. Elizabeth Warren's rally appearance on April 24, alongside Mayor Zohran Mamdani's backing. These developments have solidified Lander's grassroots coalition in the deep-blue Brooklyn-Manhattan district, overshadowing incumbent Rep. Dan Goldman's fundraising edge—including a first-quarter outperformance and April 23 pledge of at least $1 million in personal funds—and some club support like Independent Neighborhood Democrats. Lander's "People's Pledge" against dark money further energizes his base, while challengers Dan Goldman at 17%, Alexa Avilés, Yuh-Line Niou, and Cameron Kasky lag distant. Early voting begins June 13, with turnout key in this contested incumbent reelection race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Democratic victory at 93.5% in California's 26th Congressional District House race, driven by the district's Solid Democratic rating from Cook Political Report (D+9 partisan lean) and similar assessments from Inside Elections and Sabato's Crystal Ball, where Kamala Harris carried 56% in 2024. Incumbent Julia Brownley's January retirement opened the Ventura County-based seat, but five Democrats—including fundraising leader state Sen. Jacqui Irwin with nearly $500,000 cash on hand—crowd a June 2 top-two primary against four Republicans, positioning the general election on November 3 as a probable Democrat-vs-Democrat or safe Democratic hold. No district-specific polls exist, but forecasters align with historical Democratic dominance. A Republican upset would require consolidating their primary vote for a top-two berth amid a national midterm wave, scandal hitting frontrunners, or unexpectedly low Democratic turnout.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
